title: Listening Comprehension
intro: Listen to a short conversation, then answer the questions. You can replay the dialogue as many times as you want.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Hi Mr. Lee, I'm looking for a book about volcanoes for my science project.

lines.speaker: Mr. Lee
lines.text: Sure, Anna. Science books are on the third row by the window. Do you need a picture book or something more detailed?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Something more detailed, please. My project is due on Friday and I need real facts.

lines.speaker: Mr. Lee
lines.text: Then I'd recommend 'Volcanoes of the World.' It has diagrams and a glossary at the back.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Thank you! Can I also borrow a book about earthquakes? My friend Mia is doing earthquakes.

lines.speaker: Mr. Lee
lines.text: Of course. You can borrow up to four books with your library card.

questions.q: Why did Anna go to the library?
choices: To find a book for a science project
To get a new library card

questions.q: Which book did Mr. Lee recommend?
How many books can Anna borrow with her library card?

choices: Anna's friend doing a project on earthquakes

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Hey Lily, do you want to go to the park on Saturday? My dad is taking me to fly a kite.

lines.speaker: Lily
lines.text: I'd love to, but I have piano practice on Saturday morning. Can we go in the afternoon?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Sure! How about two o'clock? My dad said we can also bring sandwiches and have a picnic.

lines.speaker: Lily
lines.text: That sounds perfect. I'll bring lemonade and some cookies my mom baked.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Great! Don't forget a jacket. The weather report said it might be windy.

lines.speaker: Lily
lines.text: Good idea. Windy is perfect for kites, but I get cold easily.

questions.q: What does Ben want to do at the park?
Why can't Lily go in the morning?
Why does Ben tell Lily to bring a jacket?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Excuse me, have you seen a small brown dog? His name is Cookie and I've been looking for him for an hour.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Park
lines.text: Oh no, I haven't, but I can help you look. Does he have a collar?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Yes, a red collar with a silver tag. The tag has my phone number on it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Park
lines.text: That's smart. Where did you last see him?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Near the playground. He was chasing a squirrel and then he ran behind the trees.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Park
lines.text: Let's check the trees first, then ask the people at the ice cream shop. They notice everything that goes by.

questions.q: What is the problem in this conversation?

choices: A silver tag with a phone number

questions.q: Where did Sam last see Cookie?
Why does Mrs. Park want to ask the people at the ice cream shop?
choices: They notice everything that goes by
